header.headline: Tokenizer throughput comparison
header.subtitle: A comparison board can keep workload bands, winners, and caveats stable while payload numbers change.

legend.title: Board grammar
legend.note: Copy and measurements stay in content.json. The scene DSL only plans the board.

left.title: Workload bands
left.subtitle: The board compares short, medium, long, and very long inputs on one shared benchmark harness.
bullets: Keep every pair aligned to a shared axis
Show the workload label before the winner badge
Let the footer own benchmark conditions
left.note: The left lane sets the framing for the entire comparison.

center.title: C-Kernel trie
center.subtitle: The fast path keeps trie traversal and hot-loop memory movement lean.
bullets: Winner badges stay close to each group
center.note: The winning lane should still read as part of one fair board.

right.title: PyTorch / tiktoken
right.subtitle: The slower reference remains visible so the board reads as a fair comparison instead of a poster.
bullets: Each group shares the same workload label
right.note: A comparison board should never hide the losing reference.

metric_4.label: Key optimization
metric_4.detail: No memcpy in the hot loop

callout_1.title: Keep the board honest
callout_1.body: The slower system should remain visible even when the result is decisively one sided.

callout_2.title: Keep benchmark facts external
callout_2.body: The model chooses columns, metrics, and callouts. The payload owns numbers and benchmark notes.

footer.note: Benchmark facts stay in payload. The successor DSL should only encode board structure, style, and reusable comparison components.
